title: Hardest-impostor margin loss: the field must outrank its best lookalike, not just light up the truth
hypothesis: The §6 metric is bound by ranking quality, and the objective family is the one lever nobody has pulled. The iter-12 probes of the exp-25 champion show the true cell is the field's top-1 on only 2% of eval crops while making the top-20 (of 1024) on 33–58% — in every lighting bucket including midday — with median peak mass 0.14 and ~2 separated modes; decode, rotation, and operating-point mechanisms are ruled out. The eight consecutive reverts (26–33) all changed what FEEDS the ranking (3× trunk capacity, stride-8 readout tap, lighting dispatcher, learned map raster) or how its output is thresholded (fenced/risk calibration), or the input statistics (exp 33 — whose 1e9 is an infrastructure artifact: munich training was OOM-killed and the run was never scored). The training signal that CREATES the ranking has been pure likelihood since exp 2: Gaussian-smeared CE spreads its wrong-cell gradient across 1024 cells in proportion to softmax mass, and the σ=1.5-cell smearing makes near-misses cheap, so no gradient ever concentrates on the single distant lookalike that currently outranks the truth — which is precisely the measured failure (truth present in the candidate set, outranked at the top). Visual place recognition trains this exact problem with hardest-negative margin objectives (NetVLAD/FaceNet family). A per-crop hinge that demotes the current best-scoring impostor cell should convert top-20 presence into top-1 rank and raise peak mass, improving median kept error across all 24 area×bucket cells rather than gaming one operating point.
method: ONE change, entirely inside model/model.py's loss_fn (training-only; forward, export, train.py untouched): add a hardest-impostor margin hinge on the same C4-voted logits the decode uses. Per crop: s_pos = max logit within Chebyshev radius 1 of the true cell (3×3 block — a 128 m crop legitimately straddles ~220 m cells), s_neg = max logit over cells at Chebyshev radius > 3 (outside the 7×7 ring, so the hinge never fights the σ=1.5 CE bump), loss += 0.5 · relu(1.0 + s_neg − s_pos). Margin 1.0 logit ≈ 20× mass ratio after the β=3 decode sharpening. CE, coord L2, conf BCE, sampler, schedule, calibration, and the ONNX graph are unchanged, so the deployment gates and the operating-point calibration procedure are identical to the champion's.
expected_outcome: Worst-case median kept error improves from 797.91 m to 600–750 m, with the improvement spread across all area×bucket cells (same calibration procedure → comparable coverage, so the comparison is not an operating-point fluke). Mechanism-level predictions: probe-comparable top-1 rate (berlin midday, matched protocol) rises from ~2% to ≥8%, median sharpened peak mass from ~0.14 to ≥0.25. Falsification fork: if ranking metrics improve but kept medians do not, the residual wall is descriptor information content, not the objective — redirecting future iterations at the trunk/descriptor rather than the loss or the operating point.
eli5: The model scores 1,024 map tiles for each camera frame and we fly toward the highest-scoring one. Until now training only rewarded giving the right tile a decent score — like a teacher handing out partial credit but never checking who came FIRST. Measurements show the right tile usually makes the model's top-20 list, yet almost never ranks #1: some lookalike district across town keeps beating it. The new rule is a police-lineup rule: on every training example, find the one impostor tile the model currently likes best and demand the true tile's neighborhood beat it by a clear gap. The specific impostor that keeps winning gets pushed down, the truth pushed up.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: a slim extra layer condenses the 8×8 feature grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the texture average (48 numbers) is kept alongside

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: two scorers read each of the four turned copies and the gate blends their per-cell guesses; the four resulting heat maps are averaged into one voted map — a location only stays hot if it wins from every direction

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model inspects the shape of its own heat map — one crisp hotspot means sure, a washed-out smear means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing; the bar is set so it still answers at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: besides the usual grading (reward probability near the true spot), every training example now runs a lineup check: find the one far-away map tile the model currently scores highest and require the true tile's 3×3 neighborhood to beat it by a fixed gap — the specific impostor that keeps winning is pushed down, the truth pushed up

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: every pass draws a fresh 6,000 places per lighting condition, each rendered by one of three simulator re-rolls — unchanged

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: unchanged — 24 fresh-draw passes with the cosine learning-rate glide kept from round 25

implementation_brief:
ONE file changes: model/model.py, and within it ONLY the loss_fn function plus four new module-level constants. model/train.py is NOT modified in any way; no other file is touched.

1) Add module-level constants directly under GOOD_ERR_UV:
   RANK_POS_R = 1    # positive set: Chebyshev radius 1 (3x3) around the true cell — a 128 m crop straddles ~220 m cells
   RANK_NEG_R = 3    # negatives: Chebyshev radius > 3 (outside 7x7), clear of the TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS=1.5 CE bump
   RANK_MARGIN = 1.0 # in logit units; ~e^3 ≈ 20x mass ratio after DECODE_BETA=3 sharpening
   RANK_WEIGHT = 0.5

2) In loss_fn(pred, logits, target_uv): keep the existing ce, coord_loss and conf_loss computations byte-for-byte, then before the return add (note loss_fn already computes k = GRID_K and has cell_u/cell_v on logits.device; reuse k):
   dev = logits.device
   gx = (target_uv[:, 0] * k).floor().long().clamp(0, k - 1)   # true cell, u axis
   gy = (target_uv[:, 1] * k).floor().long().clamp(0, k - 1)   # true cell, v axis
   cell_gx = torch.arange(k, device=dev).repeat(k)              # flat idx i -> i %% k
   cell_gy = torch.arange(k, device=dev).repeat_interleave(k)   # flat idx i -> i // k
   cheb = torch.maximum((cell_gx[None, :] - gx[:, None]).abs(),
                        (cell_gy[None, :] - gy[:, None]).abs())  # [N, K*K]
   s_pos = logits.masked_fill(cheb > RANK_POS_R, float('-inf')).max(dim=1).values
   s_neg = logits.masked_fill(cheb <= RANK_NEG_R, float('-inf')).max(dim=1).values
   rank_loss = torch.relu(RANK_MARGIN + s_neg - s_pos).mean()
   and change the final line to:  return ce + coord_loss + 0.3 * conf_loss + RANK_WEIGHT * rank_loss

The flat-index geometry MUST match _grid_centers: cell_u = centers.repeat(k) means flat index i has grid-x = i % k and grid-y = i // k; the cell_gx/cell_gy construction above encodes exactly that — do not transpose it. Both masked sets are always non-empty for GRID_K=32 (the positive set contains the true cell; the negative set has ≥ 1024−49 cells), so the max never returns -inf and no NaN can arise. `logits` are already the C4-voted logits returned by forward(..., return_logits=True) — apply the hinge to them as-is: no extra forward passes, no new tensor larger than [N, 1024] (memory headroom is tight on this host — exp 33 died of an OOM kill during munich training; do not enlarge batches or the sampler). Update loss_fn's one-line docstring to mention the hardest-impostor hinge.

Fixed contracts to preserve (restated): train.py CLI stays `python -m model.train --area <a> --out-dir <d> [--data-dir <d>] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket N] [--seed N]`; the export contract in model/model.py's docstring stays: one ONNX per area at <out>/models/<area>.onnx taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] and returning [[u, v, conf]] with u, v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1], passing pipeline/score.py's frozen gates (ONNX ≤ 4 MiB, host latency proxy ≤ 250 ms). Do NOT touch forward(), _c4_stack, export_onnx, estimate_position, calibrate_conf_shift, or any frozen file. The exported graph is structurally identical to the champion's, so gates and calibration behavior are unaffected by construction.
